w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh wp14

w:r.w:t: The system must ensure strict data isolation between tenants (store owners), so that no user can access another user's data. All operations must be performed within a clearly defined tenant context.
Every API request must include a valid tenant context
All database queries must be scoped using
Any request without tenant context must be rejected
Database Layer: Use a shared schema with a mandatory
through all controllers and service methods
Middleware: Extract tenant context from authentication tokens (
Data Access Layer: Enforce query filtering using
Security Controls: Validate ownership before granting access to any resource

The system must provide responsive performance for user interactions, particularly during AI-assisted store creation. The initial AI-generated store draft should be delivered within an acceptable response time to ensure a smooth user experience.
Initial AI-generated store draft must be returned within
System response time for standard operations should remain within acceptable limits under normal load
Long-running AI operations must not block user interaction
The AI-assisted store generation process operates as a
with the AI service to deliver the initial store draft within the target response time of
To achieve this performance target, the system applies the following strategies:
The AI generates a limited initial draft consisting of store metadata and a small number of mock products (2–4 items) to reduce processing time.
The system sends a single generation request to the AI service after collecting the necessary user input, avoiding multi-step orchestration and reducing latency.
Backend services perform minimal processing during the synchronous request, focusing on validation and persistence.
The system displays loading indicators while the AI generation request is being processed.
If the AI service exceeds the acceptable response time, the system triggers the fallback mechanism defined in the availability requirements.
The system ensures secure handling of AI-generated content and multi-tenant data. Unexpected content from AI responses is explicitly defined and handled according to the type of issue
AI response does not match the expected JSON structure or is missing required fields
; triggers Partial Regeneration or Fallback Flow
Response contains invalid JSON or cannot be parsed
Profanity, hate speech, or inappropriate language detected in text fields
; system flags, sanitizes, or replaces the content before displaying
AI suggests products/categories outside the store scope
Filtered before persistence; flagged in audit logs; optionally regenerated
AI accidentally generates personal or tenant-specific sensitive data
Blocked and not persisted; triggers alert to monitoring system
to ensure that no cross-tenant data is exposed.
User inputs are sanitized to prevent injection attacks or malicious prompts.
Responses are first validated against the
Additional content moderation filters run to detect offensive or irrelevant content.
All endpoints enforce tenant context; cross-tenant operations are blocked.
Every AI generation event is logged with tenant info, including incidents of unexpected content, for monitoring and traceability.
The system must support increasing workloads without degrading performance by scaling specific components based on their responsibilities and load characteristics. The
is the primary scalability concern due to concurrent AI generation requests from multiple tenants, while the
must scale to handle increasing numbers of user requests. The
must also scale to support growing data volumes across multiple stores
AI Service: Must handle concurrent AI generation requests from multiple tenants while maintaining the 3–5 seconds response time for the initial draft
Backend Services: Must support multiple simultaneous user requests without noticeable increase in response
API Gateway must scale horizontally as entry point.
Database: Must handle increasing numbers of stores, products, and AI-generated data without performance degradation
Must allow adding instances of AI service, backend services, and API Gateway without architectural changes.
Deploy the AI service as stateless instances
Use a load balancer to distribute AI generation requests
Scale horizontally by adding more instances based on request load (e.g., high concurrent AI requests)
Design backend services as stateless to allow multiple instances
Scale horizontally to handle increased user traffic and tenant requests
Use read replicas to handle high read loads
Optionally partition data by tenant or store to reduce contention
Ensure AI processing load is isolated from core backend operations
Prevent AI-heavy requests from degrading overall system performance

The system must remain available and usable even if the AI service fails or becomes slow. The system should not block the user due to AI dependency and must provide an alternative way to continue store creation.
If the AI service fails or exceeds the response time (3–5 seconds), the system must provide a fallback option within
The user must always be able to continue the store creation process without interruption
System uptime for core services (backend + API) should be at least
If AI fails or times out
Templates include predefined layout, categories, and placeholder content
The user may retry AI generation later when the service becomes available.
User may receive results later when ready
Backend is stateless to improve availability
Failures in AI service do not crash or stop the main system

The system must provide a simple and intuitive user experience that allows store owners, including non-technical users, to easily create and manage their stores. The AI-assisted features should be easy to use, with clear guidance and minimal user effort required to generate and customize a store.
Users must be able to create a store using AI within a few simple steps (3–5 steps maximum)
The system must provide clear feedback during AI processing (e.g., loading indicators or status messages)
Users must be able to understand and edit AI-generated content without confusion
Navigation between main features (store creation, editing, management) must be straightforward and consistent
In case of AI failure, fallback options (templates) must be clearly presented and easy to use
Streamlined store creation process (minimal required inputs)
Guided steps for AI-assisted creation (e.g., description
Show loading indicators during AI processing
Inform users when fallback (template) is used
Allow users to easily modify AI-generated store data (text, categories, etc.)
Use consistent layout and navigation across the system
Provide user-friendly error messages instead of technical errors

The system must be easy to maintain, extend, and modify over time. The architecture should support clear separation of concerns, allowing developers to update or add features (such as AI capabilities or store management functions) without affecting other parts of the system.
System components must be modular, with clearly defined responsibilities (e.g., UI, controllers, services, data access)
Changes in one module (e.g., AI service) must not require changes in unrelated modules
New features (e.g., new AI capabilities) can be added with minimal impact on existing code
Codebase should follow consistent structure and naming conventions
Time required to implement a small feature or fix should be minimal (relative to system size)
Each layer has a single responsibility
Isolate core functionalities into services (e.g., AI Service, Store Service)
Changes in AI logic do not affect other services
Follow consistent project structure (especially in Django apps)
Use reusable components and avoid duplication
Keep AI integration isolated in a dedicated service/module
easily without affecting the rest of the system
